topics: Introduction and Basic Concepts
Properties of Fluids: Density, Specific Weight, Vapour Pressure, Viscosity, Newton's Law of Viscosity, Surface Tension and Capillarity, Ideal and Real Fluid
Pressure and Its Measurement: Fluid Pressure at a Point, Pascal's Law, Pressure Variation in a Fluid, Gauge Pressure, Absolute Pressure, Vacuum Pressure
Measurement of Pressure: Piezometer, Manometers (Simple U Tube, Differential and Single Column), and Mechanical Pressure Gauges

Fluid Statics: Total Pressure and Centre of Pressure, Pressure Forces on Plane and Curved Surfaces, Buoyancy and Floatation, Metacentre and Metacentric Height (Determination - Experimental and Analytical Methods)
Principle of Flotation and Stability of Floating and Submerged Bodies

Eulerian and Lagrangian Approaches: Types of Fluid Flow (1-D, 2-D, 3-D), Steady, Unsteady, Uniform, Non-uniform, Laminar, Turbulent, Rotational, Irrotational Flows, Translation, Rotation, Circulation and Vorticity, Vortex Motion (Free Vortex and Forced Vortex)
Continuity Equation: Continuity Equation in Three Dimensions, Velocity and Acceleration
Streamlines, Path Lines, Streak Lines, Stream Function, Velocity Potential and Flow Net
Dynamics of Fluid Flow: Euler's and Bernoulli's Equation of Motion, Application of Bernoulli's Equation (Venturi Meter, Orifice Meter, Nozzle Meter and Pitot Tube)
Other Flow Measuring Devices: Orifices and Mouthpieces, Notches and Weirs (Classification, Flow Through Them and Hydraulic Coefficients)
Energies in Flowing Fluid: Pressure Head, Dynamic, Static and Total Head

Flow Through Pipes: Laminar and Turbulent Flow, Head Loss Due to Friction, Major Energy Losses (Darcy Weisbach Equation, Chezy's Equation, Moody's Chart), Minor Losses (Head Loss Due to Pipe Fittings - Description Only)
Hydraulic Gradient and Total Energy Line
Flow Through Network of Pipes: Pipes in Series, Pipes in Parallel, Equivalent Pipe
Power Transmission Through Pipes

Siphon, Water Hammer and Cavitation (Description Only): Open Channel Flow, Types of Flow in Channels, Velocity Distribution, Measurement of Velocity in Channels (Current Meter, Parshall Flumes, Cut-Throat Flumes and Venturi Flumes), Chezy's and Manning's Equation
Most Economical Section of Channels: Rectangular, Trapezoidal, Triangular and Circular Channel
Specific Energy, Critical Depth and Hydraulic Jump
Pumps: Water Lifting Devices

Different Types of Pumps, Classification of Pumps, Component Parts of Centrifugal Pumps: Priming, Pump Selection, Installation and Troubleshooting, Performance Curves, Effect of Speed on Capacity, Head and Power, Effect of Change of Impeller Dimensions on Performance Characteristics, Work Done, Efficiencies, Cavitations, Characteristics Curves, Specific Speed, NPSH
Multistage Pump, Axial Flow/Propeller Pumps, Mixed Flow Pumps and Their Performance Characteristics
Deep Well Turbine Pump and Submersible Pump
